methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Lumina Vehicles is a seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Ahmed Shubber and Federico Di Palma. The relevant comp universe spans 9 total peers—1 public (277810.KQ) and 8 private entities including 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics—though this broader sector set lacks sufficient depth in the seed-stage, narrow-focus category.

The peer-median method is not applicable here. The analysis identified 11 same-sector peers in Robotics & Manufacturing, but filtering for stage-appropriate and valuation-tier-aligned comparables yielded fewer than three datapoints. Relying on an all-sector median would introduce substantial noise and misalign risk profiles typical of seed-stage frontier technology companies versus more mature robotics players. No alternative triangulation methods are supported by the provided data.

The absence of sufficient narrow-peer coverage materially constrains valuation precision. While the 9-firm comp universe provides directional context on sector multiples and funding patterns, deriving a specific valuation range requires either: (1) expansion of stage-specific comparables within Robotics & Manufacturing, or (2) introduction of methodology grounded in revenue multiples, DCF assumptions, or follow-on funding benchmarks not present in this dataset. Key uncertainties include technology differentiation, market timing, and capital efficiency relative to the broader peer set.
